##NAMES=Elliottite
##RRUFFID=R250050
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=NaMgAl_3_(PO_4_)_2_F_6_·9H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Tom’s phosphate quarry, Kapunda, South Australia
##OWNER=Museum of South Australia
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Aggregates of colourless pseudohexagonal platelets overgrowing minyulite and amorphous angastonite on a matrix of goethite. Closely associated with penriceite and wavellite. Type sample described in Grey et al (2022)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250050
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Na_0.56_K_0.40_Ca_0.02_Mg_1.79_Al_2.27_P_2.02_F_4.33_H_20.19_O_18.67_ ; determination of H_2_O based upon the dimorphic relationship to penriceite together with the crystal structure results
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder, with contamination from minyulite
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 12.242 b: 7.0118 c: 11.2946 alpha: 90 beta: 101.19 gamma: 90 volume: 951.1 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
